hero_subhead:
AI's taking careers. Climate's taking Miami. Fascism's taking attendance.

We wrote the Green New Deal. Now we're writing the Mission for America, the plan the next president will need on day one. We're not waiting.

Three existential crises. Interconnected. Accelerating. Each makes the others worse. There's no way to solve one without solving the others.

That's not a problem. That's the answer.
end_teaser: "Things are not fine. Let's start there."
end_headline: Things are not fine. Let's start there.
end_body:
Three crises. Each accelerating. Each making the others worse. The people who should be fixing it are arguing about whether it's real.

Most people are trying to slow the descent. Small ideas. Making the inevitable a little less bad.

That's not what we're doing.
begin_body:
The last time everything broke at once, we chose to act. Not manage. Not adapt. Act. We mobilized. Built a new economy overnight. Put millions of people to work in great jobs. Defeated fascism. Not because it was easy. Because we committed.

Time to do it again. A clean economy creates good jobs. Good jobs kills desperation. Desperation dead means fascism starved. Fascism starved means climate policy survives.

Connected problems. Connected solutions. One plan. The window is 2029.
pivot_line: The end of the world and the beginning of utopia are the same moment. Which one you get depends entirely on what you decide to do next.
mission_body: The full plan. 20+ chapters. Every sector. If you read one thing, make it this. (It's long. Sorry.)
done_before_body:
Last time the apocalypse came knocking, we built a new economy. Defeated fascism. Created the middle class. Whole industries overnight. Millions of unemployed trained into jobs that actually paid.

We didn't have to. We chose to.

We can do it again. The window is 2029. We're writing the break-glass plan now — the Mission for America.
thinktank_head: we're the think tank for the emergency
thinktank_body:
Most think tanks write policy designed to be "realistic." Small ideas that might survive a hostile Congress.

That's not us.

New Consensus builds plans for what actually needs to happen. We wrote the Green New Deal. Now we've written the Mission for America. Because "realistic" got us here. And here are three converging catastrophes with no serious plan in sight.

Nobody else was doing it. So we did.
join_body: If you're ready to help build the plan, start with the Mission for America and get in touch.
footer_tagline: An economic policy think tank for people who think "realistic" policy got us here.
